title: Eggplant Parmesan
intro: Eggplant Parmesan is an Italian American red-sauce classic that became a staple of home kitchens and neighborhood trattorias, built on layers of tender eggplant, tomato sauce, and melted cheese. This version keeps the familiar structure and comfort of the dish while using air-fried cutlets for a lighter path to browning before the final bake.

stepGroups.title: Salt the Eggplant
items: Lay the eggplant slices on a wire rack or paper towels and sprinkle both sides lightly with salt. Let them rest for 30 minutes to draw out moisture, then pat them dry thoroughly.

stepGroups.title: Flour and Rest the Eggplant
items: Place the flour in a shallow bowl and coat each eggplant slice lightly on both sides. Shake off any excess flour and let the slices rest for 10 minutes so the flour hydrates and adheres.

stepGroups.title: Bread and Chill the Eggplant
items: Place the Egg Beaters in one shallow bowl. In another shallow bowl, combine the panko, dried oregano, dried basil, and red pepper flakes. Dip each floured eggplant slice in the Egg Beaters, let the excess drip off, then press firmly into the breadcrumb mixture on both sides. Arrange the breaded slices in a single layer on a sheet pan or tray and place them in the freezer for 10 to 15 minutes to help the coating set.

stepGroups.title: Air Fry the Cutlets
items: Preheat the air fryer to 400°F. Spray both sides of the breaded eggplant slices with avocado oil spray. Arrange them in a single layer in the air fryer basket without crowding. Air fry for 8 to 10 minutes, then turn the slices, spray lightly again, and air fry for 5 to 7 minutes more, until deep golden brown and crisp. Work in batches as needed.

stepGroups.title: Assemble the Parmesan
items: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Arrange a layer of eggplant slices in a small baking dish. Spoon over a light layer of tomato sauce, then scatter over some of the low-fat mozzarella. Repeat the layers, using the sauce with restraint so the cutlets do not become waterlogged, and finish with the cheese on top.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Dish
items: Bake for 20 minutes, until hot and bubbly. Broil for 2 to 3 minutes to brown the top lightly. Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve hot.
